# Round 2 Quality Checklist: Postly Round 2 Social Extensions

**Purpose**: Validate whether the Round 2 specification is complete, clear,
consistent, and bounded before planning and implementation.
**Created**: 2026-04-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/mental/projects/Postly/specs/002-profile-replies-notifications/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based
on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all Round 2 user stories independently valuable, independently
  testable, and clearly bounded as extensions to existing behavior rather than
  re-specifications of public profile or post reading? [Completeness, Spec §User
  Scenarios & Testing, Spec §Scope Boundaries]
- [ ] CHK002 Are profile-editing requirements fully specified for display name,
  bio, avatar replacement, invalid edits, and cross-surface identity
  reflection? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-001-FR-005]
- [ ] CHK003 Are reply requirements fully specified for author ownership,
  author-only edit/delete permissions, deleted-reply placeholders, and
  unavailable-parent behavior? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2, Spec
  §BR-005-BR-006b, Spec §FR-006-FR-011]
- [ ] CHK004 Are notification requirements fully specified for follow, like,
  and reply events, unread/read state changes, and destination routing?
  [Completeness, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-012-FR-015]
- [ ] CHK005 Are continuation-loading requirements fully specified for the home
  timeline, profile post lists, and conversation views, including automatic
  loading, retry behavior, and end-of-list handling? [Completeness, Spec §User
  Story 4, Spec §BR-010-BR-010a, Spec §FR-016-FR-018]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is the phrase "in-scope surfaces" precise enough to identify
  exactly which profile, timeline, and direct-post contexts must reflect
  updated identity without reviewers inferring extra surfaces? [Clarity,
  Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Scope Boundaries]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "visible under current rules" specific enough to avoid multiple
  interpretations of which replies remain visible after a parent post or reply
  becomes unavailable? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §BR-002,
  Spec §BR-006b]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "opens that notification's destination" defined clearly enough
  to distinguish list viewing, previewing, and actual routed destination
  opening? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §BR-008, Spec §FR-014a]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "continuation point" defined clearly enough for reviewers to
  judge the intended trigger for automatic loading on each long-list surface?
  [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-017a]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Do reply ownership, editing, deletion, and placeholder requirements
  stay consistent across acceptance scenarios, edge cases, business rules, and
  functional requirements with no hidden exceptions? [Consistency, Spec §User
  Story 2, Spec §BR-005-BR-006b, Spec §FR-008-FR-011]
- [ ] CHK011 Do notification lifecycle requirements remain consistent across
  acceptance scenarios, edge cases, business rules, and functional requirements
  regarding when unread changes to read? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec
  §BR-008-BR-009, Spec §FR-013-FR-015]
- [ ] CHK012 Do avatar replacement, fallback, and failure-handling requirements
  remain consistent across acceptance scenarios, edge cases, business rules,
  and functional requirements? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec
  §BR-004-BR-004a, Spec §FR-003-FR-004]
- [ ] CHK013 Do scope boundaries, business rules, assumptions, and critical
  user outcomes consistently treat existing public profile and public post read
  behavior as baseline rather than new Round 2 scope? [Consistency, Spec §Scope
  Boundaries, Spec §BR-001-BR-002, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Critical User
  Outcomes]
- [ ] CHK014 Are ownership and authorization expectations consistent across
  profile editing, reply management, notifications, and inherited public-read
  baseline behavior? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec
  §User Story 3, Spec §BR-001-BR-005]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK015 Are the acceptance scenarios for each user story objective enough
  that pass/fail can be judged without introducing reviewer assumptions or
  planning-time rewrites? [Acceptance Criteria, Measurability, Spec §User
  Scenarios & Testing]
- [ ] CHK016 Are notification destination outcomes measurable enough for both
  available and unavailable targets, or do terms such as "relevant destination"
  and "clear not-available" still need sharper acceptance language? [Acceptance
  Criteria, Clarity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-014-FR-015]
- [ ] CHK017 Are pagination-state outcomes differentiated clearly enough that a
  reviewer can distinguish initial empty state, recoverable continuation
  failure, retryable continuation failure, and final end-of-list state?
  [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec
  §BR-010-BR-010a]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK018 Are alternate and recovery requirements covered for both reply
  failure timings: the target post becoming unavailable before submission and
  the parent post becoming unavailable after replies already exist? [Coverage,
  Spec §User Story 2 Edge Cases, Spec §BR-006b]
- [ ] CHK019 Are ownership and authorization scenarios covered for all relevant
  actors: profile owner, reply author, non-author signed-in user, and the
  pre-existing public-read audience kept out of new Round 2 scope? [Coverage,
  Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Scope Boundaries, Spec
  §BR-001-BR-005]
- [ ] CHK020 Are notification scenarios complete for empty-state viewing,
  unread-state visibility, read-state transition, and unavailable-destination
  recovery? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3 Edge Cases, Spec §FR-012-FR-015]
- [ ] CHK021 Are long-list scenarios complete for initial content, automatic
  continuation, local retry after failure, preservation of already visible
  items, and end-of-list completion? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 4, Spec
  §BR-010a, Spec §FR-016-FR-018]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK022 Are the assumptions about existing public profile/post read mode
  and inherited post visibility rules explicit enough that planners will not
  need to re-specify those baselines? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec
  §BR-001-BR-002]
- [ ] CHK023 Does the spec clearly distinguish between requirements inherited
  from existing post rules and requirements newly introduced in Round 2, or is
  that boundary still left implicit? [Clarity, Gap, Spec §Scope Boundaries,
  Spec §BR-002, Spec §FR-011]

## Notes

- This checklist evaluates the quality of the Round 2 requirements, not whether
  an implementation works.
- The current run is intentionally spec-focused because
  [plan.md](/home/mental/projects/Postly/specs/002-profile-replies-notifications/plan.md)
  and
  [tasks.md](/home/mental/projects/Postly/specs/002-profile-replies-notifications/tasks.md)
  do not exist yet for this feature.
- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
